title: centaurea leucophaea subsp. leucophaea
intro: Centaurea leucophaea subsp. leucophaea, also known as Greek mountain centaury, is a perennial plant species within the Centaurea genus, characterized by its distinctive white or pale yellow flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centaurea leucophaea subsp. leucophaea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antispasmodic agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
